NAT
Correct answer: 529408
Solution

529408

(10 + 1024) × 512 = 1034 × 512 = 529408 Bytes

Q47 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Let G = (V, E) be a connected undirected graph with n vertices and n edges. And each edge e of G carry a positive cost (or weight). Consider the following statements for finding MST of G:
S1 : Can find MST in θ(n) using DFS.
S2 : Can find MST in θ(n log n) using Prim’s algorithm.
S3 : only one edge from cycle of G need to remove to find MST.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Here DFS can be used to find the cycle, as only one cycle available in G and then remove the most weighted edge of cycle from graph. This can be done in θ(n).
So S1 is correct.

Q48 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider a network path P – Q – R between nodes P and R via router Q. Node P sends a file of size 106 bytes to R via this path by splitting the file into chunks of 103 bytes each. Node P sends these chunks one after the other without any wait time between the successive chunk transmissions. Assume that the size of extra headers added to these chunks is negligible, and that the chunk size is less than the MTU.
Each of the links P – Q and Q – R has a bandwidth of 106 bits/sec, and negligible propagation latency. Router Q immediately transmits every packet it receives from P to R, with negligible processing and queueing delays. Router Q can simultaneously receive on link P – Q and transmit on link Q – R.
Assume P starts transmitting the chunks at time t = 0.
Which one of the following options gives the time (in seconds, rounded off to 3 decimal places) at which R receives all the chunks of the file?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Q49 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following circuit with T flip-flops, NAND gate and XOR gate. Time required to complete its operation by T flip-flop, NAND gate and XOR gate respectively are 10 ns, 4 ns and 6 ns.

Minimum time period required in clock pulse is ________ ns. (Assume both levels of pulse have equal period)

NAT
Correct answer: 16
Solution

16
• Flip-flops will execute simultaneously ⇒ 10 ns.
• Then NAND and XOR gate execute simultaneously, but XOR gate takes more times (6 ns).
Total time period required = (10 + max(4, 6)) = 16 ns

Solution

656

Q51 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following languages are undecidable?

Correct answer: A, B
Solution

(a, b)
Membership and equivalence property of Turing Machine are not decidable.
Equivalence of DFA and Emptiness property of CFG are decidable.

Q56 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is true about Binary exponential backoff, a mechanism used in MAC protocols?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
It ensures that two or more nodes that experience a collision in a time slot will experience a lower probability of colliding with each there when they each retry that packet.

Q62 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statement/s is/are correct related to hazards in pipeline in a processor?

Correct answer: A, B, C, D
Solution

(a, b, c, d)
(a) When fetching of instruction and data from memory occurred simultaneously and memory is a hardware.
Stall in pipeline and reordering of instructions are ways to avoid effect of data hazards.

Q63 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following is/are correct for process synchronization in an operating system?

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)
(b) Global variables are shared among the threads of a process.
(c) A thread can go in block state even at the time of using a shared variable and other thread can use it.
(d) Peterson’s solution satisfies all requirements of critical-section problem include mutual exclusion, progress and bounded waiting.
